key: place:Twin Cities
name: Twin Cities
prompt: Location illustration for Twin Cities: The Dur Khadur unit received the Tidefall recall dispatch at midday. The commander read it, queried the Dark Council, and ordered rest but not withdrawal. The camp at four miles is standing. Caeris does not know the dispatch arrived. His second Hollow Accord letter sits unread on the admiralty desk.

key: place:Lostfeld
name: Lostfeld
prompt: Location illustration for Lostfeld: Kessa Stonebraid examined the iron case's outer runes and reported two things: the runes resist opening from within, not from without — and something has been pressing against them for longer than the case has been in Lostfeld. Ironmaul doubled the garrison and cleared the yard. Ashveil is one day away.

key: place:Open Sea
name: Open Sea
prompt: Location illustration for Open Sea: Equipment is ready. Weighted descent lines, sealed lanterns, and a deep-water sailor named Briggs are prepared for a dawn dive. Rowen has tied the iron key to his own wrist and will descend personally. The fleet holds anchor.

key: place:Tidefall
name: Tidefall
prompt: Location illustration for Tidefall: The Admiral did not emerge today. Palace staff confirmed to the senior trade councillor: a sealed document of external origin was delivered under the study door on Day 16. The councillor now has this information and three quiet allies. The prophet prays in the corridor outside the private chamber.

key: place:Faerwood
name: Faerwood
prompt: Location illustration for Faerwood: Lythara finalized her clan council strategy: she will not defend — she will offer the assembled houses a share of leverage from Tidefall's internal crisis. She needs her Nythariel envoy's intelligence to arrive before dawn. Velorn sent private messages to two of her client houses last night.

key: lore:The Recall Arrives — Unit Holds
name: The Recall Arrives — Unit Holds
prompt: Major lore event illustration for The Recall Arrives — Unit Holds: The Tidefall recall dispatch reached the Dur Khadur unit commander at midday on Day 18. He read it, sent a confirmation query to the Dark Council, and ordered his men to rest but not to strike camp. The Dark Council has not yet replied. The unit is in a state of formal receipt but non-compliance. Caeris does not yet know the dispatch arrived. He sent his second Hollow Accord letter north this morning and waits.

key: lore:Elder Ashveil — One Day Out, Still Blind
name: Elder Ashveil — One Day Out, Still Blind
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Elder Ashveil — One Day Out, Still Blind: Elder Ashveil camped overnight in the foothills north of Stonebreak. She is now one day from Lostfeld's outer gate. Her two druid wardens are experienced but Ashveil has no intelligence on Varek's condition — the Monastery's bid for the Vilefin package failed. She rides with authority but without knowledge of what she is walking into.

key: lore:Rowen — Improvised Gear Ready, First Descent Tomorrow
name: Rowen — Improvised Gear Ready, First Descent Tomorrow
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Rowen — Improvised Gear Ready, First Descent Tomorrow: The ship's carpenter produced weighted descent lines by evening. An old sailor named Briggs claimed deep-water experience off the Dreadwind cliffs. Sealed lanterns were improvised from cook-oil and tin fittings. Rowen has set the dive for dawn. He has told no one what the iron key is for. The crew knows only that the captain found something in the bronze tube worth risking their lives for.

key: lore:Shadow Court Clan Council — One Day Out, Velorn Prepares
name: Shadow Court Clan Council — One Day Out, Velorn Prepares
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Shadow Court Clan Council — One Day Out, Velorn Prepares: Velorn's bloc of five houses finalized its position statement overnight. Arkenor's lieutenant confirmed informal attendance. Lythara's recalled Nythariel envoy has not yet arrived — the Shadow Road takes a full day. She will face the council without the Tidefall intelligence she sent for. Velorn knows this.

key: lore:Ashveil at the Gate — Entity Contact Confirmed
name: Ashveil at the Gate — Entity Contact Confirmed
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Ashveil at the Gate — Entity Contact Confirmed: Elder Ashveil arrived at Lostfeld's outer gate at midmorning with two armed wardens. The Monastery relay message — 'entity contact suspected' — reached her at the foothills camp just before dawn, half an hour before she broke camp. She read it standing in the cold and said nothing to her wardens. She entered the approach road already knowing. Ironmaul met her at the outer yard, not the gate — a deliberate demotion of ceremony. She invoked Monastery override authority. Ironmaul said: 'Come and see what you are authorizing.' He took her to the garrison. Varek was awake. He looked at Ashveil and said, without greeting: 'It knows you sent someone.' She did not respond. Kessa Stonebraid stood in the corner. The stone tokens were still on the floor.

key: lore:Rowen Descends — The Chamber Opens
name: Rowen Descends — The Chamber Opens
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Rowen Descends — The Chamber Opens: At first light Rowen and Briggs entered the water. The descent took forty minutes. The structure below is larger than the vellum strip suggested — a vaulted antechamber of dark stone, barnacled but intact. A door of corroded iron stands at the far end. The iron key fit. The lock turned. Rowen pushed the door open and held his sealed lantern forward. He saw shelves. He saw objects. He saw writing on the far wall in a script neither he nor Briggs could read. He took three objects that could be carried: a sealed cylinder of pale metal, a flat disc of dark glass, and a folded cloth that did not decay despite submersion. Then the lantern began to dim. They ascended. Rowen surfaced pale and silent. He went directly to his cabin and locked the door.

key: lore:Shadow Court Clan Council — Lythara Exposed
name: Shadow Court Clan Council — Lythara Exposed
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Shadow Court Clan Council — Lythara Exposed: The clan council opened at dusk. Lythara's Nythariel envoy did not arrive. Velorn presented the position statement with five houses behind him. Arkenor's lieutenant observed in silence. Lythara offered the Tidefall leverage as promised — but without the Nythariel intelligence, she could only describe the Admiral's silence in general terms. Velorn interrupted: 'Our queen offers shares in a crisis she cannot name precisely.' Two of Lythara's minor client houses visibly did not speak in her defense when the floor opened. The session did not end in vote — clan councils rarely do — but the balance of visible authority shifted in the room.

key: lore:Dark Council Reply — Unit Holds, Tidefall Notified
name: Dark Council Reply — Unit Holds, Tidefall Notified
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Dark Council Reply — Unit Holds, Tidefall Notified: The Dark Council's reply reached the unit commander at noon: hold until contract review completes. A separate dispatch was sent to Tidefall's admiralty notifying the Admiral that the recall order is under contract review and cannot be honored without written contract-termination from the original commissioning party. Since the Admiral is sealed in his study and the junior clerk receives the dispatch, it sits alongside Caeris's Hollow Accord letter. Neither has been read by anyone with authority. The unit is now in formal contractual hold, not merely informal non-compliance.

key: lore:Varek Speaks the Name — Entity Communication Deepens
name: Varek Speaks the Name — Entity Communication Deepens
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Varek Speaks the Name — Entity Communication Deepens: At dawn, Varek addressed Ironmaul directly for the first time in three days without being spoken to first. He said a name: Ashveil had referred to no one by name in his presence since arriving, yet Varek said the name of the Warden she had written to — Aldric — without context or explanation. Ironmaul asked how he knew. Varek looked at the iron case. Kessa Stonebraid's morning rune-check showed the case surface had developed faint etchings overnight, not carved from without but formed from within — three spiral marks, new and precise. Ashveil, observing from the corner, went still. She recognized the marks. They appear in a Monastery text called the Concordance of Sealed Thresholds — a pre-institutional record considered too dangerous for general circulation. No one in Lostfeld should know that text exists.

key: lore:Rowen — The Map's Second Point Identified
name: Rowen — The Map's Second Point Identified
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Rowen — The Map's Second Point Identified: Rowen emerged from his cabin before dawn, alone, and spent the morning hours cross-referencing the cloth map against his fleet navigator's sealed charts without telling the navigator why. By midmorning he had tentatively identified a second marked point: a coastal shelf northeast of the Tidefall approaches, known but rarely visited, historically associated with old wrecks. The third, fourth, and fifth points remain unidentified. He re-sealed the map and returned to his cabin. The fleet crew is growing restless — three sailors asked the quartermaster if they were leaving today. The quartermaster had no answer.

key: lore:Caeris — The Clergy Answers, The Clock Tightens
name: Caeris — The Clergy Answers, The Clock Tightens
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Caeris — The Clergy Answers, The Clock Tightens: The High Cleric of the Twin Cities sent a reply to Caeris's inquiry by midday: he would witness a proclamation under the contingency clause, but required the text to be submitted for a twenty-four-hour review before signing. Lady Orryn sent a note at the same hour: she would give her answer at tomorrow morning's meeting. Lord Halveth arrived unannounced in the afternoon to ask if the clergy witness had been secured — he is growing anxious, does not want to be the only name on a failed document. Caeris told him: by tomorrow evening. He now has a hard deadline he created himself.

key: lore:Admiral's Clock — The Prophet Moves
name: Admiral's Clock — The Prophet Moves
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Admiral's Clock — The Prophet Moves: The Admiral did not emerge on Day 20 and submitted no written statement. One day remains before the incapacitation protocol triggers. The prophet, agitated since the previous evening's notification, gained access to the sealed study antechamber at midday — the junior clerk, unnerved, did not stop her. She stood at the closed door and spoke through it for approximately twenty minutes. No audible response came from within. She emerged and told the clerk only: 'He is present.' The councillors received this secondhand and debated whether 'present' constitutes a written statement under the protocol language.

key: place:Mountain Road
name: Mountain Road
prompt: Location illustration for Mountain Road: The Seer departed Stonebreak before dawn on a Monastery supply cart. He carries a warning he has interpreted as: 'what you hold may hold you in return' — directed at Kessa Stonebraid. He is three days from Lostfeld by mountain road. The case continues to etch in his absence.

key: lore:The Seer Departs Stonebreak — A Warning Travels Toward Lostfeld
name: The Seer Departs Stonebreak — A Warning Travels Toward Lostfeld
prompt: Major lore event illustration for The Seer Departs Stonebreak — A Warning Travels Toward Lostfeld: The Seer rose before dawn in Stonebreak, having received a vision-pressure he could not name but could not ignore — a woman's hands on cold iron, a bloodline marked by something older than curse. He interpreted this as a command to find Kessa Stonebraid and deliver a warning: that what she tends, she should cease to tend. He does not know exactly why. He packed light and joined a Monastery supply cart heading north toward the mountain road. He will not arrive in Lostfeld for three days. The case continues to etch in his absence.

key: lore:Lady Orryn Answers — The Proclamation Hinges
name: Lady Orryn Answers — The Proclamation Hinges
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Lady Orryn Answers — The Proclamation Hinges: Lady Orryn arrived at Caeris's office at mid-morning and gave her answer in person rather than by letter: she would sign, but only if a clause was added stipulating that her name could be withdrawn within ten days if the king recovered. Caeris had thirty minutes to decide. He agreed. The amended text must now go back to the High Cleric for re-review — adding approximately twelve hours to the timeline. The proclamation is alive but delayed.

key: lore:The Admiral's Dawn Deadline — The Door Opens
name: The Admiral's Dawn Deadline — The Door Opens
prompt: Major lore event illustration for The Admiral's Dawn Deadline — The Door Opens: At dawn, the incapacitation protocol deadline passed. The council chair arrived at the admiralty hall with two witnesses at first light — and found the door already open. The Admiral sat at his desk, dressed, with the two unread documents open in front of him. He had read them. He looked up and said only: 'Sit.' The council chair sat. What passed between them in the next hour was heard by no one else. The chair emerged alone and called an emergency closed session.

key: lore:Arkenor Receives Both Delegations — Shadow Court's Balance Point
name: Arkenor Receives Both Delegations — Shadow Court's Balance Point
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Arkenor Receives Both Delegations — Shadow Court's Balance Point: Lythara's envoy arrived at Arkenor's estate at dawn. Velorn's representative arrived four hours later. Arkenor received both separately, then together, and asked each side the same question: what happens to his three Gnome-pass trade routes if the other side wins. Neither delegation had prepared for that framing. Arkenor dismissed both for the day and said he would send word by evening. The Shadow Court's immediate future is in the hands of a man who thinks like a merchant.

key: place:Farrock
name: Farrock
prompt: Location illustration for Farrock: At dawn on Day 31, soldiers in Farrock's garrison began collapsing with black bile vomiting. The primary well smelled of rot. The commander's horse died in its stall. By midday forty soldiers were quarantined, the well sealed, and emergency supply requests sent to both DK and Twin Cities. The cipher fragment acquisition race has stalled. The tinker, frightened by the sickness, sold his transcriptions to the Gloomspire keeper before the garrison lockdown.

key: place:Gilgeth-Groth Border
name: Gilgeth-Groth Border
prompt: Location illustration for Gilgeth-Groth Border: Hargan Stonejaw departed Gilgeth at first light with twelve elders and twenty-four mounted warriors, riding for the Groth border ridge. He carries the caravan master's recorded testimony. He rejected Elowen's joint-consultation offer while accepting her observers as passive witnesses. He will reach Morgath's position in two days. Morgath has extended his hold order by one day, still unaware of Stonejaw's departure.

key: lore:The Farrock Sickness and the Tidefall Otters — Dual Omens Split the World
name: The Farrock Sickness and the Tidefall Otters — Dual Omens Split the World
prompt: Major lore event illustration for The Farrock Sickness and the Tidefall Otters — Dual Omens Split the World: At dawn a garrison sickness struck Farrock: soldiers collapsed with black bile, the well water turned foul, and the commander's horse died in its stall. Simultaneously, a herd of forty-odd river otters appeared on Tidefall's harbor docks, wandering calmly among the morning fishermen. Neither event has a clear natural cause. Factions that know of Caeris Thorne's otter dream and the God's prior signs are beginning to connect the signs.

key: lore:Caeris Thorne Dreams of the Otter Again — Mandate Sharpens
name: Caeris Thorne Dreams of the Otter Again — Mandate Sharpens
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Caeris Thorne Dreams of the Otter Again — Mandate Sharpens: Caeris Thorne woke before dawn from a second otter dream. The God's form was the same — upright, calm, absolute — but this time the instruction was narrower: begin the religion now, before the throne. She sat in the dark for an hour. She is running out of reasons to wait.

key: lore:Ironmaul Decides — He Will Meet Varek at Noon Tomorrow
name: Ironmaul Decides — He Will Meet Varek at Noon Tomorrow
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Ironmaul Decides — He Will Meet Varek at Noon Tomorrow: By morning Ironmaul had reached his decision: he will grant Varek's audience on Day 32, in the Thane's own hall, with Korin and two clan elders present. He sent word to the cell. Varek received the message, said nothing to the guard, and resumed his ritual practice. Ashveil's second letter is now one day from Stonebreak.

key: lore:Stonejaw Departs Gilgeth — Rides for Groth Border
name: Stonejaw Departs Gilgeth — Rides for Groth Border
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Stonejaw Departs Gilgeth — Rides for Groth Border: Hargan Stonejaw departed Gilgeth at first light with a mounted escort of twelve elders and Clan Stonejaw warriors. He carries the caravan master's recorded testimony. He will reach the Groth border ridge in approximately two days. Morgath's clan captains are still holding — three days left on Morgath's withdrawal order.

key: place:Stonebreak
name: Stonebreak
prompt: Location illustration for Stonebreak: Both of Ashveil's documents arrived by midday. The Senior Council read them in afternoon session and voted four to one to dispatch a formal envoy. Senior Druid Pellin Thornvale was appointed and will depart at dawn Day 33 with a Council letter asserting institutional authority and demanding Varek's transfer to Monastery custody.

key: lore:The Varek Audience — Lostfeld's Reckoning Begins
name: The Varek Audience — Lostfeld's Reckoning Begins
prompt: Major lore event illustration for The Varek Audience — Lostfeld's Reckoning Begins: Grand Druid Varek was brought before Thane Ironmaul in the clan hall at midmorning, wearing clean clothes and carrying nothing. He spoke for twenty minutes without pause. He claimed the entity beneath Lostfeld had chosen him as its voice and that the seals were not imprisonment but invitation. Ironmaul listened without interrupting. When Varek finished, Ironmaul said three words: 'You will stay.' Korin and the clan elders said nothing. The Runewardens scribes recorded everything. By afternoon, Torvek Runewarden returned from the deep shaft with a disturbing report: two of the seven inscription seals showed recent fissure — not broken, but changed.

key: lore:Ashveil's Documents Arrive at Stonebreak — Senior Council Convenes
name: Ashveil's Documents Arrive at Stonebreak — Senior Council Convenes
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Ashveil's Documents Arrive at Stonebreak — Senior Council Convenes: Both the sealed transcript and Ashveil's second letter arrived at Stonebreak by midday courier. The Senior Council read them in session through the afternoon. By evening they had agreed on three things: the binding candidate situation is real, Ironmaul's detention of Varek is an overreach, and a formal envoy must be dispatched. The envoy will depart on Day 33.

key: lore:Seer Delivers First Formal Otter Interpretation to Admiral Ver Meer
name: Seer Delivers First Formal Otter Interpretation to Admiral Ver Meer
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Seer Delivers First Formal Otter Interpretation to Admiral Ver Meer: In Tidefall's harbor-facing council chamber, the Seer gave his interpretation under formal witness: the otters are not an omen of threat but of presence — a being that watches, not hunts. He said the sign appeared simultaneously in Farrock's sickness and Tidefall's harbor, and that both were manifestations of the same source. The Admiral listened, asked no questions, and dismissed the Seer. Within an hour he had ordered Isolde to compile everything the harbor annals held about the God's prior signs.

key: lore:Stonejaw Column — One Day from Morgath's Ridge
name: Stonejaw Column — One Day from Morgath's Ridge
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Stonejaw Column — One Day from Morgath's Ridge: Stonejaw's mounted column camped at the corridor's edge overnight and will reach the Groth border ridge by tomorrow morning. His advance rider returned with the Glenhaven observer positions. Morgath's captains remain in place — the extended hold order has three days remaining. The confrontation is one sleep away.

key: place:Aeloria
name: Aeloria
prompt: Location illustration for Aeloria: The day advanced under local simulation while the prophecy engine was unavailable.

key: lore:The Clock Moves Without Prophecy
name: The Clock Moves Without Prophecy
prompt: Major lore event illustration for The Clock Moves Without Prophecy: A quiet day passes without fresh divine insight. Existing tensions and political momentum continue shaping Aeloria.

key: lore:Stabilization: Dreadwind Isles — Open Sea
name: Stabilization: Dreadwind Isles — Open Sea
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Stabilization: Dreadwind Isles — Open Sea: Dreadwind Isles committed resources to stabilize Open Sea.

key: lore:Stabilization: Groth Clans — Groth
name: Stabilization: Groth Clans — Groth
prompt: Major lore event illustration for Stabilization: Groth Clans — Groth: Groth Clans committed resources to stabilize Groth.
